items.title: Start from domain questions, not column arithmetic
items.avoid: Multiplying every numeric pair because PolynomialFeatures exists.
items.prefer: List hypotheses ("promo matters more on holidays") and encode only those interactions or ratios.
items.why: Purposeful features align with measurable business mechanisms and stay maintainable in production.
items.example: For retail, add promo_x_weekend—not forty cross-terms among inventory SKUs with no causal story.

items.title: Keep train and serve formulas identical
items.avoid: Different pandas logic in notebooks vs production SQL with divergent null or unit handling.
items.prefer: Centralize feature definitions in tested functions or dbt/SQL models versioned with the model artifact.
items.why: Training-serving skew is a top cause of silent production degradation after offline success.
items.example: Wrap price_per_sqft in def add_price_features(df): ... and call it in both batch training and the API preprocessor.

items.title: Respect point-in-time for aggregates
items.avoid: Lifetime customer stats computed over the full orders table for historical training rows.
items.prefer: Cutoff-aware groupby filters or merge_asof so each row sees only past-eligible events.
items.why: Future information in aggregates is one of the most common leakage paths in tabular ML.
items.example: For each training row with as_of_date, aggregate orders where order_date <= as_of_date only.

items.title: Match transform to distribution and model
items.avoid: Feeding heavy-tailed revenue raw to linear regression without compression.
items.prefer: log1p on skewed counts/revenue, clipping before ratios, cyclical encoding for periodic fields.
items.why: Domain transforms stabilize gradients for linear models and make tree splits more meaningful.
items.example: np.log1p(df['monthly_revenue']) before LinearRegression when revenue spans $10 to $500k.
